title: AST SpaceMobile Announces BlueBird 6 Launch: Largest Commercial Communications Array
text: BlueBird 6 is the largest commercial communications array ever deployed in low Earth orbit, spanning nearly 2,400 square feet, three times larger than AST SpaceMobile's previous record-breaking BlueBirds 1-5. BlueBird 6 was successfully launched into orbit by India's ISRO on a LVM3 rocket from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre on December 23, 2025. BlueBird 6 is engineered to deliver commercial and government applications, including high-speed 4G and 5G space-based cellular broadband directly to standard, unmodified smartphones. This represents a major innovation in satellite communications, as ASTS aims to build the first satellite network capable of connecting standard mobile phones directly from space without special terminals needed. The direct-to-cell approach is widely viewed as a game-changer in the satellite broadband industry. The company expects five orbital launches by the end of Q1 2026, with launches occurring every one to two months on average to reach 45–60 satellites launched by the end of 2026.
